description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, flat stone fragment with a pale gray to off-white base color, featuring distinct fossilized plant imprints on its surface. The stone has a rough, natural texture with visible stratification and some angular edges, suggesting it was broken from a larger piece. The fossil imprints, resembling fern or leaf patterns, are dark brown to black and are distributed across the main face, giving the stone a unique, decorative appearance. The sides are much thinner and show the stone's layered composition, while the back face has additional fossil impressions and two small adhesive labels. The surface appears matte and unpolished, with minor chips and weathering visible along the edges. This stone would likely be found as a decorative or educational feature in an urban park, botanical garden, or museum setting.
description: A flat, pale gray stone fragment with fossilized plant imprints and rough, natural edges. The surface is matte and features distinct dark leaf patterns.
description_view_0: The main face displays prominent fossilized fern or leaf imprints on a pale gray background, with rough, jagged upper edges.
description_view_1: A thin side profile reveals the stone's layered structure and irregular thickness, with minimal visible surface detail.
description_view_2: The reverse face shows additional fossil imprints, a large leaf impression, and two adhesive labels, one orange and one white.
description_view_3: Another thin side profile, similar to view 1, highlighting the stone's narrowness and stratified composition.
attribute_location: urban botanical garden or educational park
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on a display plinth or embedded in landscaping
attribute_pattern: Distinct fossilized plant imprints, primarily fern or leaf shapes, with irregular distribution and high visual contrast against the stone's pale background.
attribute_weathering: Edges are chipped and slightly worn, with a generally matte, unpolished surface indicating moderate natural weathering.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured urban landscaping or display settings, drawing attention due to its fossil patterns and irregular shape.
